english: bdúd-kyis bréd-na (bdud kyis bred na) if you are afraid of the devil Glr.; bred-˚toms (bred 'thoms) Lex.; *dḥé-po* (*dhe po*) fearful, frightful, terrible C.

I. vb. (not the same as ˚brél-ba ('brel ba)) 1. to be employed, busy, engaged, to have business or work on hand, ñed mkár-las-kyis brél-nas loṅ mi ˚dug (nyed mkhar las kyis brel nas long mi 'dug) being engaged in building, we have no time to spare Mil.; ˚dod kó-nas brél-na ('dod kho nas brel na) if one is entirely taken up with lust and pleasure; *dhe-riṅ ṅá-la dḥél-wa yo', saṅ-nyiṅ šog* (*dhe ring nga la dhel wa yo', sang nying shog*) to-day I have a great deal to do, come to-morrow C.; brél-bas (brel bas) on account of much business Dzl. - 2. synon. with póṅs-pa (phongs pa) to be poor, to be without, wanting, destitute of, c. instrum.: loṅs-spyod-kyis brél-ba (longs spyod kyis brel ba) Dzl. ༧༢, 7; more frq. with a negative: čis kyaṅ mi brél-bar byás-so (cis kyang mi brel bar byas so) they did not let him want anything Dzl. ༧༢, 17, Sch.; ˚tsó-bai yo-byád-kyis mi brél-bar ('tsho ba'i yo byad kyis mi brel bar) abounding in every necessary of life Dzl. ༧༨, 3 (acc. to a better reading); combined with another word: póṅs-brél-te (phongs brel te); brél-poṅ-méd-čiṅ (brel phongs med cing) Dzl., mi brél-bar (mi brel bar) not sparingly, scantily, niggardly, e.g. to bestow Dzl. frq. - II. sbst. 1. C. and B., a being engaged in a multiplicity of business v. I, 1. - 2. W.: business, affair, concern, *ṅá-la dḥél-wa žig yod* (*nga la dhel wa zhig yod*) I have some particular business, concern, suit; *dḥél-wa či yod* (*dhel wa ci yod*) what do you want, what are you about, what are you doing there?
1., W. also brés-kyu (bres kyu) manger; rta-brés (rta bres) manger for horses. - 2. v. bre (bre). - 3. v. ˚bré-ba ('bre ba).

1. oath, bro-˚tsál-ba (bro 'tshal ba) to take an oath (?) Pth., bro ˚bór-ba (bro 'bor ba) id., dbu-bsnyuṅ daṅ bro bór-ro (dbu bsnyung dang bro bor ro) Glr. they swore by their heads, nif. - 2. dance, bro skráb-pa (bro skrab pa) Lex., kráb-pa (krab pa) Mil., brdúṅ-ba (brdung ba) Glr., resp. żabs-bró mdzád-pa (zhabs bro mdzad pa) Mil. to dance, leap, gambol, as a manifestation of gladness and mirth, whilst gar byéd-pa (gar byed pa) is a regular kind of dancing, with gentle and waving motions of the body; rṅa-bró (rnga bro) drums and dancing Glr.; bró-mk̇an (bro mkhan) Cs. dancer. - 3. Pur. bro (bro) v. brá-bo (bra bo). - 4. v. bró-ba (bro ba). - 5. bro-nád (bro nad) Lex., Mil. and elsewh.; Sch. 'an epidemic disease'; bro-˚tsál (bro 'tshal) Sch. 'cold (in the head), cough, catarrh;' Tar.: págs-pa lo maṅ-por sku-bro ˚tsal-te (pags pa lo mang por sku bro 'tshal te); Mil.: šiṅ-tu bro-˚tsál-bar gyúr-nas (shing tu bro 'tshal bar gyur nas).

I. vb. 1. to taste, to smell, vb. a. & n.; ynyíd kyaṅ mi bró-bas (gnyid kyang mi bro bas), not even enjoying (tasting) sleep Dzl.; k̇á-ro skyú-ba bro (kha ro skyu ba bro) one has an astringent taste in the mouth Med.; spos bro-o (spos bro o) it smells of incense Dzl.; dri-ysúṅ żím-pa bro-o (dri bsung zhim pa bro o) it has a pleasant smell Dzl. - 2. C. to desire, to wish, = ˚dód-pa ('dod pa), bló-bro-ba (blo bro ba) id.; ṅu bró-ste (ngu bro ste) being about to weep Mil. - II. sbst. taste, savour, flavour, col. bro-blág (bro blag) (*ḍob-lág* (*dob lag*)), láṅ-tsa k̇a-zás kún-gyi bró-ba skyed (lang tsha kha zas kun gyi bro ba skyed) salt imparts flavour to any kind of food S.g.; bro ltá-ba (bro lta ba) or myoṅ-ba (myong ba), col. *ḍob-lag nyaṅ-če** (*dob lag nyang ce*) W. to taste, to savour; to try the taste; bró-ba-čan (bro ba can) Cs., *ḍób-lag-čan** (*dob lag can*) W. savoury, pleasing to the organs of taste, exciting the appetite; bro-(ba-)med (bro ba med) tasteless, insipid Cs.
= bró-ba (bro ba), taste (żím-po (zhim po)) *dḥi'-čeṅ* (*dhi' ceng*) C., *ḍód-čan* (*dod can*) W., well-tasted, savoury; *dḥo' čém-po* (*dho' cem po*) C. of a strong, powerful taste.
joy, joyfulness, bród-pa skyéd-pa (brod pa skyed pa) Mil.; dga-bród (dga' brod) id. C.; či-bród (ci brod) readiness to die Mil. - Here may be quoted also drod (drod) 2 and 3.
1. v. bro (bro) 5; bros-tèbs (bros thebs) Sch. - 2. v. ˚brós-pa ('bros pa).

I. the space over, above a thing, chiefly occurring in compounds; blá-na (bla na) above Lex.; bla-na-méd-pa (bla na med pa), अनुत्तर, having nothing higher over it, the upper-most, the very highest, e.g. byaṅ-čúb (byang chub), šes-ráb (shes rab) and the like frq; bla-na-méd-pai lam (bla na med pa'i lam), bla-méd-rdo-rjei tėg-pa (bla med rdo rje'i theg pa), = sṅágs-kyi lam (sngags kyi lam), the mystical method, v. mdo (mdo) 3; sá-bla (sa bla), above the earth, above ground, opp. to sa-stéṅ (sa steng), sa-˚óg (sa 'og) upon and under the earth. Generally fig.: superior, better, preferable, baṅ-mdzód stoṅ yaṅ blao (bang mdzod stong yang bla'o) then even an empty treasury is preferable Dzl.; commonly with the pf. root of a vb.:
